text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: Safari has a built-in "Translate this page" feature. When a user has it enabled (or triggers it manually) while the datepicker's calendar popup is open, Safari's translation pass walks the DOM and rewrites text nodes inside whatever is currently on screen -- including the open calendar dialog. That DOM rewrite fights with React's own reconciliation of the same nodes: users report the calendar breaking, freezing, or throwing errors on any interaction (clicking a day, changing month, etc.) once the page has been auto-translated by Safari while the calendar was open.

The calendar popup is exactly the kind of live, interactive, non-prose UI that should never be rewritten by a browser's page-translation pass -- unlike static article text, mutating it out from under React corrupts the widget. Browsers support an opt-out for this on a per-element basis; the calendar dialog element should use it so Safari (and other browsers with similar auto-translate features) leaves the calendar's DOM alone entirely.

Fix the library source so the rendered calendar dialog element opts out of browser auto-translation. Do not just add a workaround in a test file -- change the actual component that renders the calendar dialog.

Environment notes: package manager is Yarn (Berry, via corepack) -- already installed and `yarn install --immutable` already run for you. Existing tests can be run as a regression check with `NODE_ENV=test yarn test` (or `NODE_ENV=test node_modules/.bin/jest` to run the whole suite directly). This is a TypeScript + React codebase; the calendar dialog markup lives under `src/`.

sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local
---Date: 2026-07-23T04:46:36+00:00---

When a user has it enabled (or triggers it manually) while the datepicker's calendar popup is open, Safari's translation pass walks the DOM and rewrites text nodes inside whatever is currently on screen -- including the open calendar dialog. That DOM rewrite fights with React's own reconciliation of the same nodes: users report the calendar breaking, freezing, or throwing errors on any interaction (clicking a day, changing month, etc.) once the page has been auto-translated by Safari while the calendar was open.

The calendar popup is exactly the kind of live, interactive, non-prose UI that should never be rewritten by a browser's page-translation pass -- unlike static article text, mutating it out from under React corrupts the widget. Browsers support an opt-out for this on a per-element basis; the calendar dialog element should use it so Safari (and other browsers with similar auto-translate features) leaves the calendar's DOM alone entirely.

Existing tests can be run as a regression check with `NODE_ENV=test yarn test` (or `NODE_ENV=test node_modules/.bin/jest` to run the whole suite directly). This is a TypeScript + React codebase; the calendar dialog markup lives under `src/`.
I’ll inspect the calendar dialog component and its existing tests, make the smallest source change to opt the dialog out of translation, then run the required build and a focused regression check.

input.cmd:
pwd && rg -n "calendar|dialog|aria-modal|translate|Calendar" src | head -200 && printf '
--- files ---
' && rg --files src | head -100 && printf '
--- status ---
' && git status --short

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

`rg` is unavailable in this checkout, so I’m using the repository’s standard fallback tools to locate the dialog implementation and verify the current worktree. The only pre-existing changes shown are `package.json` and an untracked `AGENTS.md`; I’ll leave them untouched.

input.cmd:
find src -type f -maxdepth 3 | sort | head -150; printf '
--- matches ---
'; grep -RInE "aria-modal|dialog|Calendar|calendar" src --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' | head -240; printf '
--- package scripts ---
'; sed -n '1,220p' package.json; printf '
--- status ---
'; git status --short

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

input.cmd:
sed -n '1,180p' src/calendar_container.tsx; printf '
--- tests ---
'; sed -n '1,150p' src/test/calendar_container.test.tsx; printf '
--- config ---
'; find . -maxdepth 2 -type f \( -name 'AGENTS.md' -o -name 'tsconfig*.json' -o -name '.prettierrc*' \) -print -exec sed -n '1,180p' {} \;

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

The live popup is rendered by `CalendarContainer`, and its non-inline branch already carries `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"`. I’ll add the standard HTML `translate="no"` attribute to that container so the opt-out is present on the actual dialog, including time-only calendars; inline calendars will receive the same attribute because the container is still the calendar UI root. I’ll also extend the component regression test to lock the DOM contract.
Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

The focused test passed against the unchanged source, but the shell image does not provide `apply_patch`, so that edit was not applied. I’m applying the same minimal change with the available file-edit tool now, then I’ll rerun the focused test and the required production build.
Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]
Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

input.cmd: NODE_ENV=test yarn test src/test/calendar_container.test.tsx --runInBand && npm run build
